items.title: Key observatory shut for months prior to bird flu detection

children.content: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

gallery.altText: A large number of galas sitting on a branch

thumbnail.altText: A large number of galas sitting on a branch

items.title: Why does Perth have an obsession with double brick homes unlike any other city?

children.content: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

image.altText: A couple smile to the camera at a house construction site.

items.title: More rain on the way for WA after storm damages roofs, downs trees

children.content: Perth residents are bracing for more wet weather after a powerful cold front damaged roofs and downed trees on Friday night.

image.altText: A tree lies across the roof of a house with SES crews in attendance

items.title: 'Special place' portraits capture essence of aunties and their stories

children.content: A photography exhibition in Albany shares the stories of Menang elders, who often have been overlooked in history.

items.title: Couple welcomes refugees to home 'like family' in resettlement program

children.content: Margaret River residents Anne and John Roberts's motto is to offer a "soft landing" and a "warm embrace" to refugees as they settle into life in Australia.

image.altText: A woman in a blue floral top standing next tot a man in a brown 3/4 zip fleece holding cups of tea on a verandah

items.title: Police investigate two deaths linked to fatal crash in WA's Midwest

children.content: Detectives are probing the deaths of a woman in a crash and a man who was found dead in a hotel a day later.

image.altText: A large stone sign which says "Meekatharra" in front of red dirt.

items.title: Andrew 'Twiggy' Forrest warns Fortescue staff after sexual harassment class action

children.content: In an email sent to all staff, Mr Forrest said: "Anyone who thinks this behaviour has a place at Fortescue is in the wrong company."

articles.title: Women allege sexual assault, underwear theft, drink-spiking at Fortescue mine sites

children.content: Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest's mining giant Fortescue has been slapped with a landmark class action alleging systemic sexual harassment, discrimination and toxic work sites.

image.altText: A graphic image depicting two shadow figures in hard hats with torn red and orange background.

gallery.altText: Andrew Forrest in high-vis shirt and white hard hat folds his arms across his chest, October 24, 2025.

thumbnail.altText: Andrew Forrest in high-vis shirt and white hard hat folds his arms across his chest, October 24, 2025.

items.title: Battle lost for Zempilas and WA Liberals in hope of winning war ahead

children.content: Basil Zempilas has had his first brush with the political truism "disunity is death" after MP Jonathan Huston quit the WA Liberal Party, but he's unlikely — at this stage at least — to be the one left in a political grave.

articles.title: Nedlands MP Jonathan Huston quits WA Liberals amid One Nation hints

children.content: Elected to represent the Liberal heartland seat of Nedlands just last year, Jonathan Huston announces he is quitting the party.

image.altText: A man at an event wearing a Liberal hat.

gallery.altText: Basil Zempilas is speaking to media with Jonathan Huston in the background.

Jonathan Huston looks at the camera in his office holding papers.

items.title: Butters coy about Power future after starring in upset Showdown win

children.content: Following a masterful display, Zak Butters is awarded the Showdown Medal in what may be his last match for the Power against the Crows.

image.altText: Zak Butters wearing the Showdown Medal.

items.title: WA's second largest city to pay $1.2 million to truck food waste 160km

children.content: Bunbury ratepayers are set to foot the bill in a bid to resurrect Western Australia's three-bin FOGO waste management system from collapse outside of Perth.

image.altText: A green fogo bin mascot next to a recycling bin mascot and a landfill bin mascot outside  near trees

items.title: Fight erupts over 'heartless' decision on beach plaques for dead brothers

children.content: A family who lost two brothers to separate freak accidents is locked in a battle with a local council as they push to reinstate their beachside memorial plaques, after the benches they were on were damaged in a storm.

gallery.altText: A young man holding a surboard at a beach
A man in a white shirt poses for a photo at night

thumbnail.altText: A young man holding a surboard at a beach
A man in a white shirt poses for a photo at night

items.title: 'Glint in their eyes': How Aussies watched the Socceroos match against Paraguay

children.content: Crowds across the nation adorned in green and gold gathered in support of the Socceroos for their third match in the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: A lader and an older man each hold their Socceroo scraves and smiles, while wearing Socceroo shirts.

items.title: Another suspected H5 bird flu case detected in WA

children.content: Another suspected positive case of the H5 bird flu has been detected in Western Australia, marking the fifth confirmed or suspected case in Australia.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length hair is speaking to media.

items.title: Australia's next opponent locked in after World Cup chaos

children.content: The Socceroos will meet Egypt in the knockout stage of the World Cup after a stunning finish in Group G. Follow live.

articles.title: Egypt and Iran push back against 'Pride Match' as rainbow flags wave

children.content: Rainbow flags wave around Seattle after Egypt and Iran asked FIFA to keep a Pride celebration out of its World Cup match.

image.altText: A woman ina pride flag stands outside a stadium.

articles.title: Live table: Which third-placed teams will make it to the knockouts?

children.content: The World Cup group stage is winding down, with third-placed teams anxiously watching other results to learn their fate. See the live table to see who is on track to progress.

image.altText: The Socceroos huddle in a circle

articles.title: Departure of Leckie and Italiano leaves Socceroos 'two soldiers down'

children.content: Australia enters the World Cup knockout rounds with just 24 players in the squad after veteran Mathew Leckie and right-back Jacob Italiano departed the camp with injuries.

image.altText: A soccer player is treated for an injury

items.title: In pictures: The desperate rush to find survivors of Venezuela's twin earthquakes

children.content: After two powerful earthquakes, rescue teams from around the globe are in a race against time to pull survivors from the rubble.

image.altText: men carry an older man out of rubble

items.title: Skilled migrants want to move to this regional town, but struggle to find a house

children.content: With a wealth of job opportunities but no houses for skilled workers, a regional WA town is hoping 3D-printed homes can solve the shortage.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt and black jacket stands to the left of a woman in a brown dress, both looking at the camera.

items.title: Conspiracy theorist podcaster jailed for stabbing girlfriend to death with dagger

children.content: Tobias Nuttall is sentenced to at least 18 years behind bars for murdering his girlfriend with a 25-centimetre dagger in a "vicious" attack in her suburban Perth home.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a man with long brown hair and a moustache, talking while appearing on a video call for a podcast.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up shot of a man with long brown hair and a moustache, talking while appearing on a video call for a podcast.

items.title: Star Hawk hurt as Giants count the injury cost of stinging loss

children.content: The Hawks hold on at the MCG to beat GWS and lock up third spot for the moment, in a game marred by a number of big injuries.

image.altText: Two Hawthorn AFL players put their arms around each other in celebration after a goal.

items.title: Vulnerable teenager, young mum left stranded far from home by WA court closures

children.content: Legal advocates say a ruling requiring police to transport people refused bail to the nearest operational court is leaving vulnerable people stranded in WA's Kimberley.

items.title: H5N1 emerged in Asia 30 years ago. Here's what happened next

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

gallery.altText: A farmer in overalls feeds a flock of chickens in an enclosure.

thumbnail.altText: A farmer in overalls feeds a flock of chickens in an enclosure.

items.title: WA man jailed for killing unit manager during argument with sex worker

children.content: A West Australian man has been jailed for fatally striking a 75-year-old man who stepped in to protect his wife and a sex worker during an altercation at a Queensland apartment complex.

image.altText: A large blue sign stands in front of an apartment complex.

items.title: Coastal town braces for economic pain as mine closure to cost 100 jobs

children.content: Mineral Resources's decision to close the Lucky Bay Garnet Mine will see 110 jobs lost in and around Kalbarri.

image.altText: A picture of a mine sign saying Lucky Bay garnet mine.

items.title: In Beirut, a sacred ceremony also becomes display of Hezbollah's force

children.content: Tens of thousands of Shiite Muslims gathered in Beirut for the largest Ashura procession in years, as Hezbollah used the holy day to project strength despite growing pressure to disarm after months of war with Israel.

gallery.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.

thumbnail.altText: Dozens of men, sitting cross-legged, all of them wearing black. Some are wearing white turbans.
